title: Order parameters and ground-state phase diagram of the interacting topological Su-Schrieffer-Heeger model with extended-range hoppings
authors: Tsz Hin Hui, Pedro D. Sacramento, Wing Chi Yu
abstract: Topological insulators have attracted numerous attentions recent years, where the Su-Schrieffer-Heeger (SSH) model is one of the most studied models. While the interacting version of it has been explored recently, the interplay between interactions and long-range hoppings merit further investigations. In this work, we uncover a rich phase diagram of the interacting SSH model with extended-range hoppings, in which it consists of several topological phases, two novel superconducting-like (SC-like) phases and five distinct charge-density-wave (CDW) phases. We substantiate that the SC-like and two CDW phases are direct consequences of imbalanced interactions and extended-range hoppings. We derive the order parameters (OPs) for each of the phases and verify them in large-system simulations, finding consistency with the entanglement entropy and the fidelity in capturing the phase transitions. In contrast to the non-interacting case where the favored hoppings are unidirectional in the topological phases, the derived OPs suggest non-unidirectional hoppings are possible under the influence of interactions.
summary: This paper maps out the complex ground-state phase diagram of an extended and interacting SSH model. It identifies multiple novel CDW and SC-like phases by deriving specific order parameters for each regime. The work is significant because it shows that electron interactions fundamentally alter the nature of topological order, allowing for hoppings patterns forbidden in the non-interacting limit.
main_problem: The study investigates the rich phase diagram of the interacting topological Su-Schrieffer-Heeger (ESSH) model, focusing on superconducting-like and charge-density-wave phases.
main_result: The paper uncovers a complex phase diagram featuring multiple CDW and SC-like phases, demonstrating that interactions allow for non-unidirectional hoppings contrary to the non-interacting case.
method: Order parameters (OPs) are derived based on analyzing dominant Fock states of the ground state, and these results are verified using large-system simulations like DMRG.
model_or_system: The system is the Extended SSH Model (ESSH), described by a Hamiltonian including nearest-neighbor, next-nearest neighbor hoppings, and interaction terms ($U$ and $V$).
key_observables: Order Parameters (OPs) for various phases ($	ext{OCDW2A}$, $	ext{CSC}$), Topological invariant ($W$), Entanglement entropy, and Fidelity.
important_parameters: Interaction strengths ($U, V$), extended-range hopping parameters ($t_c, t_d$), and system size ($N$).
main_assumptions: The OP construction relies on analyzing dominant Fock states; finite-size effects are noted as a limitation for certain phases.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate phase diagrams in the $(U, V)$ plane, compare OPs with entanglement entropy and fidelity across different system sizes, and show symmetry relations between parameter regimes.
paper_structure: The work progresses by defining the Hamiltonian, deriving general OP construction schemes, mapping out the phase diagram through limiting cases of interaction parameters, and finally verifying the results using large-scale numerical simulations (DMRG).
why_interesting: The study uses advanced many-body techniques to characterize topological phases in interacting systems, providing insights into how electron-electron interactions modify fundamental superconducting or charge ordering tendencies.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Schematic diagram illustrating possible phases in var- ious limits of (U, V ). In the strong interacting regime, for any finite η, tc and td, the four phases reside at the four corners are phase separation (PS) for (U, V ) →(−∞, −∞), and dif- ferent types of charge density waves (CDWs), as detailed in the main text, for (±∞, ∓∞) and (+∞, +∞). Along the line (U, V ) ≈(λ, 0) and (0, λ), for sufficiently large λ > 0, there exists a W0 and a W1 phase, respectively, as indicated by the arrows. The central square represents the neighborhood of (U, V ) ≈(0, 0), which admits four possibilities depending on the values of η, tc and td. For |tc| and |td| sufficiently smaller than |ta| and...

FIG. 3. Ground-state expectation values of O′ W0, O′ W1, OW0, and OW1, defined in Eqs. (43), (44), (9) and (25), respec- tively. The number of hopping terms is set to n = 3 for all the OPs. Here, N = 8 and (η, tc, td) = (0.6, 0, 0). For com- parison, see the phase diagram in Ref. [71].

FIG. 2. The squared modulus of the weight |ξj|2 (see Eq. (5)) of the Fock states for ground states along U = 6 with (a) V = 5; (b) V = 2.5; (c) V = 0; (d) V = −2.5; (e) V = −5. The colormap shows the half-block entanglement entropy, with the circles marking the points at which the ground state is considered in panels (a-e) using the same color scheme. Here, N = 8 and (η, tc, td) = (0.6, 0, 0).

FIG. 4. The OPs for the possible phases in the interacting ESSH model with various td: (a) td = −0.7; (b) td = −1.5; and (c) td = −2.5. Here, N = 8, (η, tc) = (0.6, 0). O′ W0/1/2 are defined in Eq. (45), with n = 3. Cm(r) is defined in Eq. (47), and “m” can be CDW1A/2A/2B/4A or SC or PS. For the CDW family, r is set as halve of the wavelength of the corresponding phase, namely r = 1 for CDW2A/2B and r = N/2 for PS. For CDW1A, r = 0.5, which corresponds to a separation of one site. For the SC-like phase, we set r = 1 and OSC is given in Eq. (54). The bottom-right panel in each subplot shows the estimated phase diagram. FIG. 5. The log-log plot of the pairing correlation function in Eq. (55) versus the separation r measured from the middle of the chain. Blue and green markers denote the SC-like and the CDW-2B phases, respectively, with U values indicated in the legend and (η, tc, td, V ) = (0.6, 0, −1.5, −12). Triangular and circular markers denote points deep inside the phase and around the phase boundary, respectively. Data is obtained from DMRG simulation with N = 200, open boundary con- dition, bond dimension 500, and at least 10 sweeps. Dashed straight lines are linear fits to the data over r ∈[6, 25].
